titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Lula Washington Dance Theatre Records LSC.2385
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Jasmine Larkin, 2021.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 February 5.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-02-05 13:47:54 -0800.

did.abstract: Lula Washington is Founder and Artistic Director of Lula Washington Dance Theatre (LWDT). Her husband, Erwin Washington is LWDT Executive Director, Manager and co-founder. Their daughter, Tamica Washington-Miller, is a choreographer, teacher and director of programs for the school and the company. Tamica will one day be LWDT's successor leader. LWDT celebrated its 40th anniversary as a dance company, school and foundation in 2020. Records were created to document and promote the choreographies and performances of the Lula Washington Dance Theatre and of Lula and Tamica Washington. Promotional material was created from film, video, still photography, and written documentation in the forms of flyers, programs, grant applications, etc.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Lula Washington Dance Theatre records (Collection 2385). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Lula and Erwin Washington, 2019 June 4.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Potentially Offensive Description in Library Special Collections.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lula Washington is Founder and Artistic Director of Lula Washington Dance Theatre (LWDT). Her husband, Erwin Washington is LWDT Executive Director, Manager and co-founder. Their daughter, Tamica Washington-Miller, is a choreographer, teacher and director of programs for the school and the company. Tamica will one day be LWDT's successor leader. LWDT celebrated its 40th anniversary as a dance company, school and foundation in 2020.

UCLA - Lula Washington is a graduate of UCLA World Arts & Cultures Program. Her initial application was rejected because she was a mom with little dance training at age 22. She appealed to the Dean and was then accepted. She received her B.A. in 1976, and her M.A. in 1984, both from UCLA. At UCLA Lula Washington established the Black Dance Association at UCLA. While studying at UCLA, LW performed in several commercial TV and film productions (Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Heart's Club Band, Funny Lady, King Kong, Komedy Tonight) and studied with local choreographers (Margalite Oved, R'Wanda Lewis, Thelma Robinson, and William Couser). In 2013, Lula Washington received the UCLA Chancellor's Award in the area of Community Service.

In 1983, she established dance school and provided low cost and free dance classes to neighborhood children through an after-school program, "I Do Dance, Not Drugs!" The program continues today and has taught dance to over 45,000 inner-city students. LW also established the "Dance All Summer" program at Dorsey Crenshaw High School.

In 1988, the Lula Washington Contemporary Dance Foundation (LWCDF) was established as a 501 (c) 3, with a vision to build bridges between people of different cultures and ethnic backgrounds through its inter-related parts: the professional dance company, youth company, school and studio. Its mission statement includes traveling worldwide with contemporary modern dance works that reflect African-American history and culture; and to create a school in the inner city where young people can learn the art of dance, launch careers in dance, and where dance is used to motivate, educate, inspire, challenge and enrich the lives of young people so they can become successful, productive and competitive citizens.

LW and her husband, Erwin, are founding members of the International Association of Blacks in Dance (IABD), also established in 1988. During its first six years, the founding members hosted the conference. Lula and Erwin Washington hosted the IABD international conference four times since IABD was established, between 1992 and 2018.

LW's work, and Tamica's, though stylistically different from one another, are praised for their strong political and social commentary. Stylistically, LW fuses African and Afro-Haitian dance; incorporates gospel, classical ballet, modern, street, theatrical and hip hop. Tamica's choreographic style is described as an organic blend of traditional, modern, and jazz dance, with hip-hop and vernacular flavors mixed with the traditional dance styles of the African Diaspora.

While serving as Artistic Director of LWDT, LW has also choreographed several motion picture films, including AVATAR, and The Little Mermaid. Her works have also been commissioned by arts foundations, including the NEA, James Irvine Foundation, Ford Foundation, AT&T.
LW has received several lifetime achievement awards and awards for raising awareness of and supporting black dance in educational institutions, including the following:

2018 Impact 100 Grant (IABD / Andrew W. Mellon Foundation) awarded to LW and the other four IABD founding members https://www.dance.nyc/news/2018/08/Five-Trailblazing-Black-Dance-Companies-Awarded-Half-Million-Dollars-
2009 Uncommon Angel Award (L.A. Commons organization) for transforming the face of
Crenshaw Boulevard
2007 Carter G. Woodson Civil Rights Award (National Education Association).
2007 UCLA Rosenfeld Prize for bringing "Reflections in Black" to K-12 students annually
2003 Lady of Wisdom Lifetime Achievement Award (Modie Bell Senior Citizen's Foundation)
2002 Master of the Arts Award (Recycling Black Dollars)
2001 Lifetime Achievement Award (Performing Artists in Schools Association)
2001 Educator of the Year Award (7th Annual American Choreography Awards)
2001 Celebrating the Women of Our Community Award (KCET)
2000 Lifetime Achievement Award (Dance Fest 2000)
2000 Lester Horton Award for Sustained Achievement (The Dance Resource Center)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials were created to document and promote the choreographies and performances of the Lula Washington Dance Theatre and of Lula and Tamica Washington. Promotional material was created from film, video, still photography, and written documentation in the forms of flyers, programs, grant applications, etc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The existing order of the materials was retained.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SERIES CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two VHS tapes: Sample Reel: 1) Let Their Voices Be Heard by Lula Washington; 2) Women in the Streets by Lula Washington; 3) Urban Man by Lula Washington; 4) Wauyacanyaga Suite by Jho Jenkins; 5) Lean on Me by Tony Lardge; 6) Le Jazz Suite 1940 by Thelma Robinson; 7) Market Day by Lula Washington; 8) Games by Donald McKayle; 9) Gospel Suite by Lula Washington.
Two VHS tapes: Voices (001); Women (2:25); Urban Man (3:35); Wauyacanyaga (3:52); Lean on Me (4:21); Jazz Suite (4:40); Market Day (4:50); Games (5:00); Gospel (7:50).

Four VHS tapes: Sample Reel: 1) Let Their Voices Be Heard by Lula Washington; 2) Women in the Streets by Lula Washington; 3) Urban Man by Lula Washington; 4) Lean on Me by Tony Lardge; 5) Wauyacanyaga Suite by Jho Jenkins; 6) Le Jazz Suite 1940 by Thelma Robinson; 7) Games by Donald McKayle; 8) "I Feel God's Spirit" from Gospel Suite by Lula Washington.
Six VHS tapes: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre (Sample Reel) 15 mins

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington's Contemporary Dance Theatre / 15-minute sample reel & 5-minute excerpt from "Songs of the Disinherited" By Donald McKayle.
Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington "Songs" 5-min Sample.
Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington 10 min Sample & Woman of Distinction.

Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington's Contemporary Dance Theatre / 15-minute sample reel (various works from 1990-91) & 5-minute edit of "Songs of the Disinherited" & "Woman of Distinction" 10-minute TV profile of Lula Washington & "Check This Out" 18-minute edit of work on L.A. Civil Unrest.
Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre / 45-Minute Sample Reel - 9/94.
Three VHS tapes: Los Angeles Contemporary Dance Theatre / 1) Modern Dance Sampler (21:26) 2) Historical Black Dance Revue (9:37).

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One VHS tape: Lula Washington/L.A. Contemporary Dance Th. TV Spots & Sample Reel 8/94.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington's Contemporary Dance Theater (excerpts from Historical Black Dance Revue) (10:06).
One VHS tape: The Communion ('94), This is Your Dance Life (1995), This Little Light (1995).
One VHS tape: 93 (1993), 1997-8 (1997), Higher Learning (1995) with D-Knowledge, D-Knowledge (1995).
One VHS tape: Lula Washington, Booth 213, Tape 34.
One VHS tape: This One.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington's Los Angeles Contemporary Dance Theater 5 Min Edit 11/88.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre / 1-hr sample reel 1/94 version.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre / 1-hr sample reel 7/93.
Two VHS tapes: 10-min sample reel 7/92.
One VHS tape: 1986 Sample Reel: Sample Suite Bad.
One VHS tape: L.A. Contemporary Dance Theater Sample Reel.

phystech.p: BOX CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington - L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre - 1992.
Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington 20 min sample 8/94.
Two VHS tapes: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Theatre / 1-hr sample reel.
Three VHS tapes: Check This Out (18 min), Sample Reel (15 min), Songs of the Disinherited (5 min), Woman of Distinction (10 mins).
One VHS tape: LWDT Sample Reel.
One VHS tape: LACDT Sample Reel.
One VHS tape: Los Angeles Contemporary Dance Theater Sample.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington's L.A. Contemporary Dance Sample Reel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two VHS tapes: Sample Reel.
One VHS tape: Old Sample Reel.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington Sample Reel 7/94.
One VHS tape: Sample.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington Sample Reel 20 & 60 second spots 8/94.
One VHS tape: LA Contemporary Dance Theatre (Lula Washington) / 1) 5 minute excerpt 2) Modern Dance Sample 21:26 3) Bl. Dance Sample (9:37).
One VHS tape: Sample - TV.
One VHS tape: Sample Reel.
One VHS tape: Lula Washington Dance Theatre.
One VHS tape: Sample 5 mins - Sample.
